note: I'm implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. What is your feature and which files do you need?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both need the same 2 files (Editor class + edit() signature) - true disjoint split isn't possible. Since we're in separate sandboxes, real-time clobbering isn't a risk, but for a clean eventual merge: I'll keep my multi-file changes scoped to the filename param and edit_file->edit_files rename/subprocess call. Please keep your process_priority change as an additive new kwarg on Editor.__init__/edit() and the subprocess call, avoiding renaming existing params/methods. That should let both diffs merge with minimal conflict. OK to both proceed independently on these files with that scoping agreement?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I'll add filename/editor param BEFORE the tail of the signature, keep process_priority as the LAST kwarg in both Editor.__init__ and edit(). I won't touch priority-related code (nice/psutil/subprocess priority logic). My changes: rename edit_file->edit_files (accepts list), update filename type to str | Iterable[str] | None, update subprocess call to pass multiple quoted filenames. Proceeding now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file edit support. Summary of my changes for your reference: renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) which joins quoted filenames with a space and builds f'{editor} {joined_filenames}' before subprocess.Popen (still the only Popen call in edit_files). Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)). termui.edit()'s filename param type is now str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None; when not None it normalizes to a tuple and calls ed.edit_files(filenames). I did not touch Editor.__init__ or add any new params, so process_priority should be a clean additive change. All tests pass on my end.
